page.title: Wait — do I qualify? A two-minute check
page.promise: Your answers never leave your device. Nothing is stored. This is a two-minute estimate, not a decision — the programs decide.
page.verifiedAsOf: Numbers last verified {date}. Sources listed on every card.
form.householdSize: How many people live in your household?
form.income: Household income before taxes (a close guess is fine)
form.flags.vehicle: There's a car in the household
form.flags.failedSmog: …and it failed its last smog check
form.flags.pregnant: Anyone pregnant in the household, or a baby at home?
form.flags.medicare: Anyone in the household on Medicare?
form.flags.disability: Does anyone in the household have a disability?
form.enrolled: Already enrolled in any of these?
form.submit: Show me what I likely qualify for
results.strong: Strong matches — worth applying this week
results.likely: Likely matches — apply, let the program do the math
results.notNow: Not right now — but know the move
results.empty: We didn't find a strong match from your answers — but screeners miss things. Call 2-1-1: a real person will go deeper, free.
reason.under-limit: A household of {household} at {income}/month is under this program's {limit}/month limit
reason.unlock: Being enrolled in {program} already passes this program's income test
reason.age: Someone in your household is in this program's age range
reason.member-flag: Someone in your household matches the group this program serves
reason.universal: This one is for everyone in California
reason.senior-net-test: You're over the {limit} gross limit, BUT households with someone 60+ only face a net-income test — apply and let the county do the real math
reason.flag-match: This applies to your household's situation — read the note and make the call
reason.waitlist-closed: The waiting list is closed right now — the card shows the honest next move
reason.income-screen: Your income looks low enough that a call is worth it — the county sets, and doesn't publish, the exact limits
reason.income-band: Your income is in the range where this credit may apply — the exact cap depends on filing status and number of kids
reason.own-income: This program counts only the applying person's own income (and their spouse's), not the whole household's — being over the household limit doesn't rule it out
note.senior-net-test: 60+ in the home? Only net income counts — ask when you apply.
note.student-rules: College students have extra rules — work, work-study, or parenting can qualify you.
note.expedited-3-day: Almost no money right now? You may be entitled to CalFresh within 3 days — if your monthly income is under {limitExpedited} and you have {limitLiquid} or less on hand, or if your rent plus utilities cost more than your monthly income and money on hand combined. Migrant and seasonal farmworker households with {limitLiquid} or less also qualify. Say 'expedited service' when you apply.
note.wic-caregivers: Dads, grandparents, and foster parents can enroll a child.
note.wic-pregnant: Pregnant? You count as a household of two — enroll as early as you can.
note.nid-care-chain: Getting CARE (above) is itself a ticket to this water discount.
note.clca-liability-only: Liability-only coverage — it keeps you legal, it doesn't cover your own car.
note.clca-benefit-proof: Already on CalFresh, CalWORKs, or Medi-Cal? Your EBT card, award letter, or Medi-Cal card counts as income proof — one less document to hunt down.
note.retirement-failed-smog-tier: The {amountFailedSmog} no-income-test tier requires a failed smog check; income-qualified, a car that passed still gets up to {amountIncomePass}.
note.medical-baseline-device: For households where someone relies on a powered medical device; a doctor/NP/PA signs the first enrollment.
note.freed-any-age-disability: Any age with a disability qualifies too — check the disability box above and this card follows. Call.
note.lsnc-senior-soft: 60 or older? Just call — there's a senior program and intake will tell you what you qualify for.
note.eitc-annual-basis: These are yearly tax credits — eligibility uses your total income for the tax year, so it's worth checking even if this month's pay looks different.
note.eitc-kid-count-bands: Limits depend on kids + filing status. With no kids at home: federal cap {limitSingle} (single) / {limitMarried} (married); CalEITC cap {limitCaleitc} either way.
note.eitc-itin: No SSN? ITIN filers still get CalEITC and the Young Child Tax Credit (not the federal EITC).
note.eitc-investment-cap: Investment income over {limitInvestment} (2025) disqualifies the federal EITC.
note.eitc-retro-years: Missed past years? You can still claim refunds — federal up to 3 years back, CalEITC up to 4.
note.calworks-earned-disregard: If you're working, the first {amountDisregard} of each worker's monthly pay doesn't count at application — apply even if you're a little over.
note.calworks-pregnant: Pregnant with no kids at home yet? You can qualify at any stage of pregnancy — ask.
note.calworks-region: Limits shown are for Nevada County (Region 2); many urban counties are slightly higher.
note.calworks-assets: Savings/property over {limitAssets} ({limitAssetsSenior} if someone is 60+ or disabled) can affect eligibility — your home and most cars don't count.
note.ga-call-first: The county sets GA amounts and income limits and doesn't publish them — call 530-265-1340 and ask before writing it off.
note.ga-last-resort: GA is only for people who can't get CalWORKs or SSI — it's the safety net under the safety net.
note.ga-repayable-work-rules: Usually a repayable grant (you may have to pay it back), and employable adults must register with Cal-JOBS and show weekly job search.
note.medi-cal-year-round: Medi-Cal enrolls year-round — no open-enrollment window, apply any day.
note.medi-cal-65plus-assets-2026: 65+ or disabled? A similar income limit applies through a different pathway, but starting 2026 savings/property over {limitAssets65} (single; +{amountPerPerson} each additional person) count (your home and car don't).
note.medi-cal-immigration-2026: 2026 rule change: people already enrolled keep their coverage, but new full-scope enrollment for undocumented adults isn't open right now. Emergency and pregnancy care are still open to everyone, and kids' coverage doesn't depend on status.
note.medi-cal-kids-any-status: Children qualify regardless of immigration status.
note.medi-cal-kids-to-19: Kids' Medi-Cal actually runs to the 19th birthday — if your 18-year-old needs coverage, ask.
note.mcap-213-322: Over the free limit? MCAP covers pregnancy at low cost up to about {limitMcap}/mo for a household of 2 (2026).
note.pregnancy-any-status: Pregnancy coverage is open regardless of immigration status.
note.cc-enrollment-window: Open enrollment is Nov-Jan. Right now you can still enroll within 60 days of a qualifying change — losing coverage or a job, moving, marriage, a baby. Medi-Cal has no window.
note.cc-subsidy-cliff-2026: 2026 change: the extra pandemic-era credits ended Dec 31, 2025 — above ~400% of the poverty level there's currently no discount, and many middle-income premiums rose.
note.cc-state-subsidy-2026: New for 2026: California state help keeps premiums near last year's levels for incomes up to about {limitStateSubsidy}/mo (single).
note.cc-medi-cal-handoff: Under about {limitMediCal}/mo (single) you likely belong in free Medi-Cal instead — same application, they route you.
note.msp-own-income: MSP counts only the Medicare member's own income (and spouse's) — not everyone in the house. If grandma's own income is under about {limitOwnIncome}/mo, she may qualify even in a bigger household.
note.msp-tiers: Which tier you land in depends on income: under about {limitQmb}/mo (single) the QMB tier also erases Medicare deductibles and copays; the county works out the right tier.
note.msp-assets-2026: New for 2026: savings/assets over {limitAssets} (single; +{amountPerPerson} each additional person) count again. Your home and main car don't.
note.ssi-aged-disabled: SSI is for people 65+, or blind or disabled at any age — the income numbers here apply to that person, not the whole house.
note.ssi-own-income: Only the applicant's own income (and spouse's) counts — a low-income elder in a bigger household can still qualify.
note.ssi-earned-disregards: If you work, most of your paycheck doesn't count (the first {amountDisregard} plus half the rest) — it's worth applying even if you earn above the numbers here.
note.ssi-resources: Savings over {limitResourcesSingle} (single) / {limitResourcesCouple} (couple) can disqualify — but your home and usually one car don't count.
note.s8-watch-reopen: The list reopens with little notice — announced in area newspapers and at regionalha.org. Check monthly, and dial 2-1-1 to ask about any housing list openings.
note.s8-eli-priority: When the list opens, most new spots go first to the lowest incomes (under about {limitEliSingle}/mo for one person, {limitEliFour} for four).
note.s8-property-waitlists: Separate from Section 8, some apartment communities keep their own low-income lists — for Nevada County properties, call the property manager and ask.
note.s8-other-counties: Moving out of the county? Your new county's housing authority runs its own list — find it via hud.gov's PHA lookup or 2-1-1.
note.meals-no-application: There's no form and no income test — every public and charter TK-12 student can just eat, every school day.
note.meals-public-charter-only: Applies to public school districts, county offices of education, and charter schools (private schools set their own programs).
note.nemt-book-5-days: Call at least 5 days before your appointment — (866) 828-2303, Mon-Fri 7am-7pm. Door-to-door (NEMT) needs a form signed by your provider.
note.nemt-nmt-no-other-way: Regular rides (NMT) just need you to confirm you have no other way to get there — no license, no working car, or you can't travel alone.
note.sunbucks-apply-deadline: On CalFresh or CalWORKs? Kids 6–18 get the card automatically. Otherwise apply AT YOUR CHILD'S SCHOOL by {deadline} — ask the office for a Universal Benefits Application.
note.sunbucks-universal-meals-trap: Free school meals for everyone ≠ SUN Bucks. Even if your child eats free at school, CDSS says apply anyway.
note.sunbucks-use-it-122-days: Spend it within {daysToUse} days of the money loading — expired SUN Bucks can't be replaced. If you also get CalFresh, spend SUN Bucks first.
note.wap-call-to-apply: Apply by phone: (916) 782-3443, Mon-Fri 9am-4pm (Project GO, the local provider for Nevada County).
note.wap-priority-populations: Priority goes to the lowest incomes (below ~75% of the poverty line), high energy bills, and households with seniors, disabilities, or young children — but anyone under the income limit can apply.
note.wap-any-fuel: Not a PG&E program — wood, propane, and oil-heated homes qualify too.
note.wap-renters-ok: Renters qualify too — WAP serves low-income homeowners and renters alike; the provider arranges your landlord's OK before work starts.
note.headstart-foster-homeless-regardles: Children in foster care and families experiencing homelessness qualify regardless of income — say so when you call.
note.headstart-local-contact: For Nevada County, the local operator is KidZCommunity: 530-886-4122. Ask about both Head Start (3-5) and Early Head Start (pregnancy to 3).
note.headstart-waitlist-ask: No space right now? Ask to be added to the waiting list — families move up as slots open.
note.sfmnp-season-window: 2026 season: cards are usable {seasonStart}–{seasonEnd}. Apply early — benefits are first come, first served and run out.
note.sfmnp-first-come: You must apply in person each season through your county's local agency — call 916-214-1381 to start.
note.sfmnp-authorized-farmers: Not every stall takes the card — look for the 'WIC and Senior Benefits Welcome' sign.
note.calkids-newborn: Baby born in California on or after July 1, 2022? An account is already open — worth up to {amountOlder} (born July 2022–June 2023) or up to {amountNewer} (born after July 1, 2023) with the claim and ScholarShare-link bonuses. Use the Local Registration Number (LRN) on the birth certificate; allow 90 days after birth.
note.calkids-student-cohort: Public-school students get {amountStudent} if the school identified them as low-income or an English learner (grades 1–12 in 2021–22, or 1st grade in 2022–23 or later) — plus {amountFosterHomeless} more each for foster and homeless youth, up to {amountStudentMax}. Check with the student's SSID; if a young child isn't found yet, check again when they reach 2nd grade.
note.calkids-claim-free: Checking and claiming are free and take minutes — nothing is deducted. You can't add money to a CalKIDS account, but linking a free ScholarShare 529 lets family chip in.
note.genbat-fire-area-gate: This rebate is only for addresses in a Tier 2/3 High Fire-Threat District, a High Fire Risk Area, or on an EPSS outage-prevention circuit — most of western Nevada County qualifies. Check your address on PG&E's Wildfire Safety Progress Map first: a green bubble means you pre-qualify.
note.genbat-buy-first: It's a reimbursement, not a coupon: buy a qualifying product first (CARB-compliant generator or a 290–1,000 Wh portable battery from PG&E's qualified list — USB power banks don't count), keep the receipt and delivery confirmation, then apply online. {amountBase} back, or {amountCareFera} total if you're on CARE or FERA. One per household.
note.genbat-sunset: The program ends soon: applications must arrive within 12 months of purchase or by {deadline}, whichever comes first.
note.reach-shutoff-notice-required: REACH is for the moment things break: you need a past-due balance of at least {limitBalance} (only {limitBalanceSenior} if you're 62+) AND a 15-day or 48-hour shutoff notice in hand. If you're on a payment plan (AMP), REACH can't stack with it.
note.reach-once-per-year: Grants run up to {amountMax}, sized to your situation, once per calendar year, first come first served while funding lasts — apply the day the notice arrives, not the day the power goes out.
note.reach-local-agency: Local help exists: Salvation Army Grass Valley (530-274-3500, Mon-Thu 10am-3pm) walks you through the application and the documents — recent bill + income proof for everyone in the home. Pioneer Community Energy customers qualify too.
note.p10m-proof-list: Proof is ONE of: your CalFresh EBT card (front and back), a Medi-Cal eligibility letter (printable from BenefitsCal — use the letter, not the plastic card, which can be rejected because it doesn't say 'Medicaid'), an NSLP school-meal eligibility letter, or a letter from your child's CEP school. Documents must be from the last year, and the parent/guardian name on the application must match (add a photo ID if the document doesn't show your name).
note.p10m-universal-meals-trap: Free school meals for everyone in California ≠ proof. If you're not on CalFresh or Medi-Cal, ask your child's school office for a school-meal (Universal Benefits) application — the eligibility letter it produces is accepted proof.
note.p10m-one-per-household: One hotspot and one data line per household no matter how many students; data resets every year on your activation anniversary for 5 years. If you run out mid-year, an optional {amountDataPass}/10GB pass exists — but the free data itself never costs anything.
footer.threeDoors: Whatever the screener says: 2-1-1 (24/7, English/Spanish) · findhelp.org · our Discord — real people, free.
note.lifeline-carrier-signup: You sign up once, on the phone company's own website — the company manages the state application for you.
suggest.heading: More help worth knowing about
suggest.intro: Not part of the check above — these are pointers from our free-resources guide that match your situation. Each opens in a new tab.
note.childcare-need-reason: You also need a reason for care — working, looking for work, in school or job training, seeking housing, or unable to care for your child yourself. Kids in protective services or at risk also qualify.
note.childcare-24-month: Once you're in, you stay eligible for at least 24 months before the next income check.
note.childcare-special-needs-13plus: Children 13 and older with an IEP and a professional's letter can also qualify.
note.childcare-how-to-apply: Ask Sierra Nevada Children's Services to add you to the Childcare Eligibility List (CEL). On CalWORKs? Your county worker sets up Stage 1 care.
note.calworks-ha-homeless-trigger: This is for families who are homeless or about to lose their housing. If that's you, ask about it.
note.calworks-ha-temporary-rates: Up to {days} nights of motel help — {amountDailyBase}/day for a family of 4 or fewer, plus {amountPerExtra} per extra person, up to {amountDailyMax}/day.
note.calworks-ha-permanent: Permanent help can cover a security deposit and last month's rent, or up to 2 months of back rent to stop an eviction.
note.calworks-ha-once-per-year: Available once every {months} months (with exceptions like domestic violence, a medical emergency, or an uninhabitable home).
note.capi-immigration: Whether receiving cash aid affects an immigration case depends on your situation — talk to a free nonprofit immigration legal advocate (call 2-1-1) before applying if you're unsure.
note.capi-own-income: CAPI looks at your own income, not your whole household's — so you may still qualify even if you live with family who earn more.
note.capi-resources: You can have up to {limitResourcesSingle} in savings ({limitResourcesCouple} for a couple). Your home and usually one car don't count.
note.capi-blind-higher: Blind applicants get a higher amount ({amountBlindSingle} single / {amountBlindCouple} couple).
note.cccc-agency-referral: You can't apply yourself — a caseworker, school, or nonprofit that already helps your family has to send the request (with documents) to cccrequests@sncs.org.
note.cccc-covers: Grants of {amountMin}–{amountMax} for things like rent, utilities, car repairs, medical bills, counseling, sports, or summer camp.
note.cccc-last-resort: This is a last-resort fund — for when other help has run out.
note.foodbank-no-proof: No paperwork, no ID, no income proof — everyone is welcome.
note.foodbank-sites-shift: Distribution sites and dates change month to month — check the online calendar or call for what's happening this week.
note.foodbank-locations: Food is handed out at community sites, NOT the Grass Valley warehouse. Regular sites: Grass Valley (12889 Osborne Hill Rd) 2nd & 4th Thursday 10am–1pm; Truckee (12116 Chandelle Way) Tuesdays 4–5pm.
note.ifm-bring-id: Bring a photo ID and proof you live in Nevada County (a lease, mortgage, or utility bill).
note.ifm-every-other-week: You can pick up food once every other week.
note.ifm-hours: 440 Henderson St, Grass Valley. Open Mon/Wed/Fri/Sat 10am–1pm — reserve a slot Mon/Wed/Fri 10–11am (phone or online), or walk up 11am–1pm and Saturdays.
note.hotspot-card-good-standing: Any adult or teen with a Nevada County library card in good standing can borrow one (Youth, Limited, and Educator cards can't).
note.hotspot-14-day: Free unlimited data for 14 days (not renewable); one at a time, connects up to 10 devices.
note.hotspot-waiver: You sign a short waiver in person the first time; a parent or guardian signs for teens under 18. A very-overdue hotspot has a {amountReplacement} replacement charge that clears when you return it.
note.firewood-low-income: For low-income seniors 60+ who heat with wood. The household is one senior living alone, or two low-income seniors 60+ together.
note.firewood-western-nc: Serves Western Nevada County — volunteers cut, split, and deliver the wood to your home.
note.firewood-apply-before-winter: Wood is delivered for the winter — call (530) 615-4541 well before cold weather to get on the list.
note.gcss-meals-two-ways: Two ways to eat: a lunch café at the Grass Valley senior center (Mon, Tue & Thu at noon, any senior 60+ welcome) or Meals on Wheels delivered to your home twice a week.
note.gcss-meals-donation: No charge — a {amountSuggested} donation per meal is suggested, but no senior is ever turned away for not being able to give.
note.gcss-mow-need: Home delivery is for seniors who have trouble shopping or cooking — call (530) 446-6853 to sign up. The lunch café is open to any senior 60+.
note.unclaimed-always-free: Searching and claiming are always free at claimit.ca.gov — never pay a "finder" or asset locator (the law caps their fee at 10%, and you don't need one).
note.unclaimed-what-it-is: Lost or forgotten money the state safeguards — uncashed paychecks and refund checks, dormant bank accounts, insurance payouts, and utility deposits — until the owner claims it.
note.unclaimed-no-deadline: There's no deadline — the state holds it until you claim it. Search your name and any past or maiden names, and check family members' names too. Questions: (800) 992-4647.
reason.income-ami: Your income is in the range this program serves
note.rsi-three-requirements: You must meet all three: 5 or more outages since Jan 1, 2024 on a wildfire-safety (EPSS) circuit, AND be on CARE, FERA, Medical Baseline, or Self-Identified Vulnerable.
note.rsi-no-self-signup: You can't apply online — PG&E invites eligible customers by letter. If you think you qualify, get on CARE/FERA/Medical Baseline first (pge.com/care), then call Resource Innovations at 559-500-3550.
note.rsi-not-guaranteed: Meeting all three still doesn't guarantee a battery — the program is limited.
note.bptm-fire-area: For homes in a high fire-threat area (HFTD Tier 2 or 3, or a High Fire Risk Area) or on a wildfire-safety (EPSS) circuit.
note.bptm-generator-specs: You supply your own portable generator with a NEMA L14-30R plug (30 amps at 240V). The meter install is free.
note.bptm-service-specs: Your home needs 120/240V, 200-amp-or-less service and a SmartMeter, and you must own the home or have the owner's OK.
note.pbsr-outage-history: You qualify only if you've had {outages}+ wildfire-safety (PSPS) outages since {since} — check your address on PG&E's outage map.
note.pbsr-first-time-qpl: For first-time battery buyers only; the battery must be on PG&E's Qualified Product List and bought on or after Jan 1, 2025.
note.pbsr-tou-dr: You'll need to be on a Time-of-Use rate plan and an approved Demand Response program.
note.pbsr-deadline: Apply within 12 months of your battery's Permission to Operate date, or by {deadline}, whichever comes first — and while funding lasts.
note.usedev-two-tiers: {amountStandard} for any PG&E customer, or {amountPlus} if you're income-qualified or on CalFresh, Medi-Cal, SSI, WIC, CalWORKs, or LIHEAP.
note.usedev-standard-sunset: Apply within {applyWindowDays} days of buying. After {standardEndsDate}, only the income-qualified (higher) rebate remains.
note.usedev-vehicle-rules: Used battery-EV or plug-in hybrid on the HOV list, registered to your PG&E address. One rebate per person, three per address.
note.usedev-income-alt: The higher tier is also open to households under the county income limit — check the table at evrebates.pge.com/income-limits.
note.evcharge-two-tiers: Everyone gets up to 50% of the cost back; if you're under 80% of county income or on CalFresh, CalWORKs, Medi-Cal, SSI, or WIC, you can get up to {amountCharger} (or {amountPanel} with a panel upgrade).
note.evcharge-needs-ev: You need an EV already registered at your address and an active PG&E electric account.
note.evcharge-income-alt: The higher 'Rebate Plus' tier is for households under 80% of the county's area median income, or on a qualifying assistance program.
note.evcharge-one-per-household: One rebate per household, ever — first-come, first-served.
note.heehra-waitlisted: Single-family HEEHRA rebates are fully reserved statewide (as of Feb 24, 2026). New requests for Northern California — including Nevada County — go on a waitlist in case more funding opens.
note.heehra-amounts: If it reopens: up to {amountLow} for lower-income households (at or below 80% of area median income) or {amountModerate} for moderate-income (80–150% AMI), toward a heat-pump heating and cooling system.
note.heehra-scam-warning: The only IRA-funded electrification rebates in California right now are HEEHRA through TECH-certified contractors. Never give your financial statements to a contractor who promises a rebate.
note.woodstove-old-stove-gate: You qualify if you own your home and heat mainly with an old, non-EPA-certified wood stove or fireplace.
note.woodstove-voucher-range: When funded, vouchers cover up to {amountStoveStd} for a stove or {amountHeatPumpStd} for a heat pump — or up to {amountStoveEnh} / {amountHeatPumpEnh} for income-qualified households in a State-designated low-income census tract.
note.woodstove-call-funding: This program is out of funds right now — call (530) 274-9360 to ask about the next round.
note.ptp-lien-repaid: Remember: this is a loan, not a grant. The state pays your property taxes now and puts a lien on your home — you (or your estate) repay it with interest when you sell, move out, transfer title, or pass away.
note.ptp-equity-window: You need at least {equityPct}% equity in the home, and applications are only open October 1 through February 10 each year — mark your calendar.
note.ptp-household-income-def: The {limitAnnual} limit is 'household income' as the state defines it (Revenue & Taxation Code 20503) — it can differ from your paycheck total, so apply even if you're close.
note.hox-one-time-filing: You only file once — form BOE-266 with your county assessor — and the ~{amountSavings}/year saving repeats automatically as long as you own and live there. If you've owned your home a while and never filed, you're probably leaving this on the table.
note.hox-feb15-deadline: File by February 15 to get the full exemption for the year; file later and you may get a partial exemption until the next full year.
note.dor-what-you-get: Your DOR counselor builds a plan around your goals. It can pay for job training, work tools and equipment, college and textbooks, disability equipment, and support like childcare or transportation to get you working.
note.dor-no-income-test: There's no income limit — DOR services are based on your disability and your goal to work, not on how much you earn.
note.dor-how-to-apply: The online application is down right now — call (800) 952-5544 (TTY (916) 558-5673) or visit your nearest DOR office and staff will walk you through it.
note.habitat-income-floor: There's a minimum income too — you need to show you can afford the mortgage. It runs from about {amountMinOne}/yr for one person up to {amountMinEight}/yr for eight. If your income is between that minimum and the maximum for your household size, you may qualify.
note.habitat-sweat-equity: You commit {hoursRequired} hours of 'sweat equity' — helping build your home and others' — before you buy. Up to 20% can be volunteer hours done on your behalf.
note.habitat-how-it-works: Selection happens in rounds: watch for a public information meeting, then an application window opens and packets go out. You'll bring 3 years of tax returns with W-2s. Income from public assistance counts and does not disqualify you.
note.silversneakers-plan-dependent: Whether you have it depends on your specific Medicare plan — many Medicare Advantage plans and some Medigap plans include SilverSneakers, but Original Medicare by itself does not. Check your plan in seconds at tools.silversneakers.com.
note.silversneakers-whats-included: It covers gym memberships at thousands of participating locations, live and on-demand fitness classes, and a fitness app — all at no extra cost when your plan includes it.
note.dcap-apply-before-buying: Apply and get approved BEFORE you buy or lease — the grant is not retroactive.
note.dcap-grant-tiers: Up to {amountBase} toward the car (up to {amountDac} if you live in a disadvantaged community), plus {amountCharging} for charging.
note.dcap-no-prior-carb: You can't have used another California EV-purchase incentive (like the Clean Vehicle Rebate Project) before.
note.dcap-coaching-financing: Comes with free financial coaching and fair-financing options; no citizenship needed (ITIN qualifies).
note.ciap-invention-required: You need an actual invention you want to patent.
note.ciap-uspto-video: You must first watch a free ~39-minute USPTO training video ('Basic Patent Training for Independent Inventors').
note.ciap-fees-not-covered: The attorney is free, but you still pay the USPTO's filing fees.
note.miracleflights-confirmed-care: The child must have a confirmed appointment at a treatment site too far from home, and be medically cleared to fly on a commercial plane.
note.miracleflights-apply-14-days: Apply at least 14 days before travel — the treatment site submits a medical verification form.
note.miracleflights-who-flies: The child plus up to 2 parents or guardians fly free (3 passengers).
note.mma-what-you-get: Gas cards to drive yourself, bus/train tickets, or commercial flights (plus one escort if medically necessary). Gas cards and flights are nationwide.
note.mma-distance-rules: For medically necessary trips: driving 50+ miles one way (or 100+/month), or flying 200+ miles. You must be able to travel without medical supervision.
note.mma-apply-window: Apply 7-45 days before your appointment (3-4 weeks ahead is best). You, a social worker, or a case manager can request.
form.flags.student: Is anyone in your household in college or job training?
form.flags.homeless: Is anyone in your household homeless or at risk of losing housing?
reason.calgrant-income-band: Your income may be within Cal Grant's limits — they depend on your family size and whether you're a dependent, so the exact cutoff varies
note.calgrant-generous-limits: Income and asset limits apply, but they're generous — most working families with a college student qualify (a family of 4 can earn up to about {amountFamily4Annual} a year; savings under {limitAssetDependent}).
note.calgrant-fafsa-deadline: File the FAFSA or CA Dream Act Application by {deadline}, and submit a verified GPA.
note.calgrant-what-you-get: Cal Grant A up to {amountGrantA}/yr, Cal Grant B tuition + a {amountAccessB} access award; an extra ~{amountDependent}/yr if you have dependent children.
note.scef-currently-enrolled: For students currently enrolled at any Sierra College campus.
note.scef-what-covers: A one-time grant up to {amountGrantMax} for a sudden emergency — rent deposit, gas, car repair, textbooks, a medical bill — that could knock you out of school.
note.scef-safe-larger: A separate emergency fund may help with bigger barriers like housing or food — ask your campus Financial Aid or Student Life office.
note.scbus-how: Get a current ASSC Student Activity Sticker each term from your campus ASSC/Student Life office, then just show your Sierra College ID when you board.
note.scbus-routes: Works on Roseville, Placer County, and Auburn Transit, Nevada County Connects, and Tahoe-Truckee (TART). Credit and noncredit students both qualify.
note.hh-residency-checkin: Check-in is daily 4:00-5:30 p.m. — call (530) 271-7144 or come to 1262 Sutton Way, Grass Valley. Ask about county-residency rules when you call.
note.hh-what-you-get: A bed, hot meals, showers, laundry, case management, job training, and free rides (HAT van).
note.hh-no-id-pets: No photo ID required. Approved pets are welcome. Single adults and families.
